lesson.claim: The quantum error-correction preflight is a replayable local 3-qubit bit-flip stabilizer proof packet: it declares logical states, stabilizers, syndrome bits, correction map, supported error model, emits QEC_STABILIZER_FIXTURE_MATCH for no-error and single Pauli-X errors on both logical basis states, rejects or marks unverifiable five negative controls, and keeps surface-code, hardware QEC, fault-tolerant computation, quantum advantage, cryptographic, and BuildLang/buildc-native claims out of scope.
lesson.scope: Project Telos thirtieth-wave prooflesson for proof-carrying quantum-computing claims

scaffold.prompt: Restate, in your own words, exactly what this claim asserts and nothing more: "The quantum error-correction preflight is a replayable local 3-qubit bit-flip stabilizer proof packet: it declares logical states, stabilizers, syndrome bits, correction map, supported error model, emits QEC_STABILIZER_FIXTURE_MATCH for no-error and single Pauli-X errors on both logical basis states, rejects or marks unverifiable five negative controls, and keeps surface-code, hardware QEC, fault-tolerant computation, quantum advantage, cryptographic, and BuildLang/buildc-native claims out of scope." (scope: Project Telos thirtieth-wave prooflesson for proof-carrying quantum-computing claims).

Judging by their refs alone (demo/research/quantum-error-correction-source-receipts.json, demo/quantum-error-correction-proof-packet.mjs, demo/quantum-error-correction-proof-packet.test.mjs, docs/outreach/receipts/thirtieth-wave/quantum-error-correction-proof-packet-2026-07-02.json, docs/outreach/receipts/thirtieth-wave-quantum-error-correction-run-2026-07-02.json, docs/outreach/receipts/thirtieth-wave-quantum-error-correction-report-2026-07-02.md), predict what each recorded source could contribute as evidence for or against the claim.
The packet's overall verdict is MATCH. Before reading anything else, derive what a MATCH verdict must mean about the relationship between the claim and the recorded checks.
List the checks you would run to reach your own verdict on this claim, then compare your list against what the packet's verdict implies was actually checked.

retrievalQuestions.question: What evidence would falsify this claim: "The quantum error-correction preflight is a replayable local 3-qubit bit-flip stabilizer proof packet: it declares logical states, stabilizers, syndrome bits, correction map, supported error model, emits QEC_STABILIZER_FIXTURE_MATCH for no-error and single Pauli-X errors on both logical basis states, rejects or marks unverifiable five negative controls, and keeps surface-code, hardware QEC, fault-tolerant computation, quantum advantage, cryptographic, and BuildLang/buildc-native claims out of scope."?
Which recorded source would you re-check first, and what does its content hash let you prove about it?
What does an overall verdict of MATCH entitle you to claim, and what does it not?
The packet's scope is "Project Telos thirtieth-wave prooflesson for proof-carrying quantum-computing claims". Give one claim that would exceed that scope.

lesson.boundary: Lesson scaffold only -- prompts for the operator to derive the reasoning; never an answer dump, never a graded-assessment answer.
